title: Fix OAuth callback 'Actor not available' connection failure

requirements.text: Fix the OAuthCallback page so it does not show 'Connection Failed / Actor not available' when the actor is not yet initialized. The page should wait for the actor to become available (with a loading state) before attempting to exchange the OAuth authorization code, rather than failing immediately if the actor is null or undefined.

quotes: Please make sure you are logged in and try again.

acceptanceCriteria: OAuthCallback page shows a loading indicator while the actor is initializing instead of immediately failing
OAuth code exchange is only attempted once the actor is confirmed available
If the actor becomes available within a reasonable timeout, the OAuth flow completes successfully
If the actor is genuinely unavailable after timeout, a clear error is shown with a retry option
No 'Actor not available' error appears for a logged-in user who completes the Google OAuth redirect flow

requirements.text: Ensure the actor initialization in useActor hook and the OAuthCallback page are properly synchronized — the OAuthCallback should re-attempt or wait using React Query's enabled flag or a polling/retry strategy until the authenticated actor is ready before proceeding with the OAuth callback processing.
acceptanceCriteria: useActor hook readiness state is correctly consumed by OAuthCallback before making backend calls
OAuthCallback does not call backend actor methods when actor is null/undefined
A retry or wait mechanism is in place so transient actor initialization delays do not cause hard failures

constraints: Do not modify frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts
Only modify frontend/src/pages/OAuthCallback.tsx and any related hooks if needed
nonGoals: Changing the Google OAuth flow or credentials
Redesigning the OAuth callback UI beyond fixing the error handling
